summary.intent: Fix Codex review findings on PR #664 (Drawer component) by adding regression tests and patching the state machine transition logic.
summary.outcome: All four Codex review findings addressed with regression tests and a transition patch; mutation testing passed clean (188 mutants, 0 missed); follow-up commit pushed to the PR branch.

repo: Drawer state machine requires SyncProps to be enqueued before open/close events so that controlled prop sync runs first.
Drag-end nearest-snap resolution must convert dismissal progress into visible snap height before finding the nearest snap point.
Snap sync should preserve the active snap index unless the snap-point set itself changes; no-op snap transitions must not emit SnapChange.
The repo uses cargo-mutants for mutation testing and cargo-insta for snapshot testing; both are part of the required pre-push gate.
PR workflow requires applying a 'snapshot-reviewed' label when .snap files are added, posting a '@codex review' trigger comment, and polling for Codex review completion before merging.
Commit trailers use 'Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>' for AI-assisted commits.

code.finding: Snap-change predicate logic; mutation testing found surviving mutants here around 'did snap actually change' edge cases (same index/changed height, changed index/same height).
Drag-end nearest-snap math corrected to convert dismiss progress to visible snap height before resolution.
Transition patch area for controlled prop sync ordering (SyncProps before open/close).

workflow: Mutation testing (cargo-mutants) can abort with an internal 'interrupted' build-phase error that is not a code failure; rerunning resolves it.
Waiting for Codex review requires polling both PR reactions (👀 = in progress) and review threads; reactions appear before threads.
cargo llvm-cov and proptest are run as part of the review-fix validation suite before committing follow-up changes.
CI matrix includes unit, integration, check, clippy, formatting, mutual exclusion, snapshots, and error-variant coverage checks.

friction: Mutation testing run aborted mid-way with an internal 'interrupted' error, requiring a full rerun.
Codex review polling required multiple sleep/poll cycles (120s intervals) with no structured notification mechanism.
Locating the correct 'yeet' skill path required searching multiple plugin cache directories.
open_items: Second Codex review pass (@codex review trigger) still pending after the follow-up commit was pushed.
33 unviable mutants in the mutation run were not triaged — worth reviewing to confirm they are genuinely unreachable.
CI checks on the follow-up commit were not yet confirmed green at session end.
